description:
You are working as a backup-operator who is testing the nightly restore workflow.  
The system already contains two timestamped backup directories:  

• /home/user/backups/2024-05-01/  
• /home/user/backups/2024-06-01/  

At the moment, a convenience symbolic link called /home/user/backup_latest still
points to the older directory (2024-05-01).  
Your job is to retarget that symbolic link so that it correctly refers to the
newest backup, /home/user/backups/2024-06-01, and to write a one-line log file
confirming the new link target.

Required end state:
1. /home/user/backup_latest must exist as a symbolic link whose **only** target
   is the exact path “/home/user/backups/2024-06-01”.
2. A log file must be created at /home/user/restore_test.log.  
   • It must contain exactly one text line (terminated by a newline).  
   • The line must have the form:  
     BACKUP_LATEST_TARGET=/home/user/backups/2024-06-01

No extra spaces, comments, or additional lines are allowed inside the log file.
Use any valid single terminal command(s) to accomplish the task, but the final
state of the filesystem must match the description above; automated tests will
read the link’s target and the log file verbatim.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before agent starts):
- Directory: /home/user/backups/
    - Subdirectory: 2024-05-01/
    - Subdirectory: 2024-06-01/
- Symbolic link: /home/user/backup_latest ➜ /home/user/backups/2024-05-01

Expected final filesystem (after agent finishes):
- Symbolic link: /home/user/backup_latest ➜ /home/user/backups/2024-06-01   (no relative paths, no additional options)
- File: /home/user/restore_test.log
    Exact contents (including trailing newline):
    BACKUP_LATEST_TARGET=/home/user/backups/2024-06-01\n
Permissions:
- The agent (uid: user) has full read/write access to /home/user and its sub-paths.

The grading script will verify:
1. that “/home/user/backup_latest” is a symlink;
2. that readlink -f /home/user/backup_latest yields “/home/user/backups/2024-06-01”;
3. that cat /home/user/restore_test.log outputs exactly  
   “BACKUP_LATEST_TARGET=/home/user/backups/2024-06-01\n”.
name: task_000000_13e95805
